summary.what: Auto-skip reverts of documented commits from pending detection
summary.why: Revert commits genuinely add no new design intent when the reverted commit is already documented — the original entry IS the audit trail; requiring a fresh entry just inflates ledger noise.
summary.how: Added internal/ledger/revert.go that detects 'Revert "..."' subject + 'This reverts commit <sha>' body trailer (regex), cross-references the SHA against every entry's Workset.Commits, and skips the revert when documented; integrated alongside infrastructure filtering in filterLedgerOnlyCommits via a shared filterByRules helper.

notes: Reviewer required all failure modes to fall back to 'normal pending' (squashed reverts, GC'd SHAs, undocumented originals, multi-revert with mixed coverage) — chose the conservative path of 'any undocumented SHA in a multi-revert keeps it pending' to avoid silent loss of context. SHA matching uses prefix match for short-SHA tolerance even though git revert defaults to 40-char SHAs. The --revert flag for new entries was deliberately left out of scope per design review — auto-skip alone covers the common case; manual --revert can be a separate bead if real demand emerges.
